pattern: Scanning divisors up to the square root of both num+1 and num+2, keeping the pair with the smallest gap, exploits symmetry to find the closest factor pair in O(sqrt(n)).
hints: For any integer N, if d divides N then so does N/d. The pair (d, N/d) is closest when d is nearest to sqrt(N).
Check both num+1 and num+2 — each may yield a different closest pair — and pick whichever pair has the smaller absolute difference.
Iterate d from 1 up to sqrt(N) for each candidate; whenever d divides N, record (d, N/d) and compare the difference with the current best.

edge_inputs.description: num+1 is a perfect square giving the tightest pair
num+1 and num+2 both prime — only divisors are 1 and themselves
num where num+2 has a closer pair than num+1
